meta.title: Developer Ownership Funding Stack Watchlist
meta.refresh_cadence: Quarterly review, or sooner when state/local ownership program terms change.
meta.owner_decision: C3 starter set: deed-restriction/buy-down, inclusionary requirements, and DPA layering. Owner confirmation requested before treating this as the canonical developer stack.
meta.methodology: Developer-facing screening list based on EPS Phase II Table 12. Amounts are included only when visible on verified official pages; otherwise the Deal Calculator renders VERIFY and leaves the source out of applied gap-closing dollars.
meta.source_verification_note: New entries must be browser-loaded or bot-verified against the official source before commit. Public pages that do not state a dollar or percentage term must keep amount fields null and render VERIFY.

programs.name: WMRHC Good Deeds buy-down
programs.capability_summary: Buy-down model that can reduce the for-sale price in exchange for a deed restriction.
programs.screening_note: Applied as a per-unit screening cap only; confirm buyer, unit, and deed-restriction eligibility before underwriting.
programs.benchmark_note: EPS Phase II Table 12 identifies deed restriction/buy-down as a developer-side affordability tool.
programs.source_note: Official WMRHC Good Deeds page; verified by HTTP probe on 2026-07-22.

programs.name: Aspen affordable housing mitigation
programs.capability_summary: Local affordable-housing mitigation requirement that may affect project obligations or negotiated affordability terms.
programs.screening_note: Terms depend on project type, size, and local review; verify the current ordinance and fee schedule before treating this as gap-closing funding.
programs.benchmark_note: EPS Phase II Table 12 identifies inclusionary requirements as a developer-side affordability tool.
programs.source_note: Official City of Aspen page; verified by HTTP probe on 2026-07-22. Specific dollar terms are not encoded here.

programs.name: CHFA down-payment assistance layering
programs.capability_summary: Buyer-side down-payment assistance that can layer with a for-sale affordability strategy when the buyer and mortgage path qualify.
programs.screening_note: Applied as a maximum buyer-side layer in the screening stack; confirm CHFA loan, borrower, and product eligibility before relying on it.
programs.benchmark_note: EPS Phase II Table 12 identifies down-payment assistance layering as a developer-side affordability tool.
programs.source_note: Official CHFA down-payment assistance page; verified by HTTP probe on 2026-07-22.
